CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-06-2022 Continued To Jun-28-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-11-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ENZO NABIEV, Esq. of Moon & Yang, APC (Tel: 213-232-3128) for plaintiff;
Atty. ROBERT GEIGER, Esq. of Littler Mendelson, P.C. (Tel: 415-433-1940) for defendant Bauer's Intelligent Transportation, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT BAUER'S INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to compel arbitration is granted in part. The court compels arbitration of plaintiffs' individual PAGA claims and stays the remainder of this case, which implicates the representative PAGA claims. Plaintiffs concede that their individual claims are arbitrable. The court declines to dismiss plaintiffs' representative claims because they still have standing pursuant to Kim v. Reins Int'l California, Inc. (2020) 9 Cal.5th 73, 86. Plaintiffs are allegedly "aggrieved employee[s]" per Labor Code sec. 2699(a), (c). Plaintiffs do not cease to be aggrieved employees merely because their individual claims are compelled to arbitration. In Kim, the court held that an employee who settles and dismisses his individual claim still has standing to maintain a representative action. (See Kim, 9 Cal.5th at 80 ["Settlement of individual claims does not strip an aggrieved employee of standing, as the state's authorized representative, to pursue PAGA remedies."].) Similarly, employees that are compelled to arbitrate their individual claims are not stripped of standing to maintain a representative action.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Natalie Botelho, CSR No. 9897, Email: nataliecsr9897@gmail.com, Tel: 510-501-3003 (302/RBU)
